a:T3bec,<div class="specification">
<p>A bag contains <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="n" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>n</mi>
</math></span> marbles, two of which are blue. Hayley plays a game in which she randomly draws marbles out of the bag, one after another, without replacement. The game ends when Hayley draws a blue marble.</p>
</div><div class="specification">
<p> Let <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="n" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>n</mi>
</math></span> = 5. Find the probability that the game will end on her</p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Find the probability, in terms of <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="n" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>n</mi>
</math></span>, that the game will end on her first draw.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Find the probability, in terms of <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="n" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>n</mi>
</math></span>, that the game will end on her second draw.</p>
<div class="marks">[3]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.ii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>third draw.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>fourth draw.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.ii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Hayley plays the game when <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="n" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>n</mi>
</math></span> = 5. She pays $20 to play and can earn money back depending on the number of draws it takes to obtain a blue marble. She earns no money back if she obtains a blue marble on her first draw. Let <em>M</em> be the amount of money that she earns back playing the game. This information is shown in the following table.</p>
<p><img src="data:image/png;base64," style="display: block;margin-left:auto;margin-right:auto;"/></p>
<p>Find the value of <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="k" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>k</mi>
</math></span> so that this is a fair game.</p>
<div class="marks">[7]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">c.</div>
</div>b:T2fe7,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p><span class="mjpage"><math alttext="\frac{2}{n}" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mfrac>
<mn>2</mn>
<mi>n</mi>
</mfrac>
</math></span>     <em><strong>A1 N1</strong></em></p>
<p> </p>
<p><em><strong>[1 mark]</strong></em></p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>correct probability for one of the draws      <em><strong>A1</strong></em></p>
<p><em>eg   </em>P(not blue first) = <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="\frac{{n - 2}}{n}" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mfrac>
<mrow>
<mi>n</mi>
<mo>−</mo>
<mn>2</mn>
</mrow>
<mi>n</mi>
</mfrac>
</math></span>,   blue second = <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="\frac{2}{{n - 1}}" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mfrac>
<mn>2</mn>
<mrow>
<mi>n</mi>
<mo>−</mo>
<mn>1</mn>
</mrow>
</mfrac>
</math></span></p>
<p>valid approach      <em><strong>(M1)</strong></em></p>
<p><em>eg</em>   recognizing loss on first in order to win on second, P(<em>B'</em> then <em>B</em>),  P(<em>B'</em>) × P(<em>B </em>| <em>B'</em>),  tree diagram</p>
<p>correct expression in terms of <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="n" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
<mi>n</mi>
</math></span>       <em><strong>A1 N3</strong></em></p>
<p><em>eg</em>   <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="\frac{{n - 2}}{n} \times \frac{2}{{n - 1}}" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">

<p> </p>
<p><em><strong>[3 marks]</strong></em></p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.ii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>correct working      <em><strong>(A1)</strong></em></p>
<p><em>eg</em>   <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="\frac{3}{5} \times \frac{2}{4} \times \frac{2}{3}" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">

<div class="question_part_label">b.i.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">

<p><em>eg</em>  <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="\frac{3}{5} \times \frac{2}{4} \times \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{2}{2}" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">

<div class="question_part_label">b.ii.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>correct probabilities (seen anywhere)      <em><strong>(A1)(A1)</strong></em></p>
<p><em>eg</em>   <span class="mjpage"><math alttext="{\text{P}}\left( {\text{1}} \right) = \frac{2}{5}" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML">
